linkedin_bio: A fusion technology startup tackling the challenge of handling and breeding tritium fuel in fusion power plants. Kyoto Fusioneering uses advanced simulations and AI-driven multi-physics models to design tritium fuel cycle systems (e.g. breeding blankets and extraction units) that achieve self-sufficiency. Their integrated design approach employs machine learning to optimize myriad parameters – neutron flux in the lithium breeder, coolant flow for heat removal, tritium permeation rates, etc. all at the same time. kBy training on thousands of virtual blanket configurations, the AI helps converge on designs that maximize tritium production (so the reactor breeds more fuel than it burns) while ensuring tritium processing is efficient and safe.

title: Alta Vision
linkedin_bio: Developing AI-enhanced renewable mini-grids to provide 24/7 power in off-grid villages. In the AR-Mini pilot (with Robert Gordon Univ. UK), a cloud AI system forecasts solar output from a 20 kW PV + 2 kW wind setup and predicts the community’s demand using smart meter data​. ​It then optimally schedules the dispatch of batteries and a backup diesel genset. The AI effectively automates distribution control and demand-side management: e.g., it might pre-charge batteries before an evening peak or start the generator only if a prolonged cloudy period is forecasted. This intelligent control maintains reliable power while cutting diesel use by an expected.

title: Technosylva
linkedin_bio: Provides AI-driven wildfire risk modeling software to utilities in wildfire-prone regions. Technosylva’s platform ingests weather forecasts, satellite data, vegetation dryness, and the utility’s asset data, then uses machine learning to predict where utility equipment might spark fires​. ​Utilities like PG&E and SDG&E use these AI risk maps each day to inform Public Safety Power Shutoffs – selectively turning off power on certain lines when the model indicates high ignition probability. The AI also helps prioritize grid hardening (e.g. identifying which line segments to underground based on risk). By acting on Technosylva’s morning risk forecasts, utilities have prevented numerous potential fire starts and can target shutoffs more precisely, minimizing customer impact.

title: Makai Ocean Engineering
linkedin_bio: Pioneering the use of AI to improve OTEC system efficiency. Makai operates the world’s only grid-connected OTEC research plant and has implemented AI algorithms to analyze the ocean temperature gradient data and operational parameters (pump speeds, working fluid flow rates) in real time​. ​The AI model adjusts controls to respond to subtle changes – for instance, tweaking the cold water intake flow when surface water warms, or optimizing turbine load to current conditions. This results in higher net power output and more stable operation. By enhancing the energy conversion process through continuous AI optimization, Makai is making OTEC more viable for scale-up.

title: Caltech's Space Solar Power Project
linkedin_bio: Developing autonomous coordination and optimization for space-based solar power satellites. In 2023, Caltech launched the Space Solar Power Demonstrator (SSPD-1), which tests modular, foldable solar arrays that beam energy to Earth via microwaves. The system relies on adaptive algorithms (a form of AI) to align and phase the array elements for maximal power transfer. The AI optimizes the microwave beam steering in real time, accounting for satellite motion and atmospheric effects to keep the beam focused on the receiving antenna on Earth. It also manages each module’s solar collection angle to continuously gather sunlight. This intelligent control is essential for delivering continuous, reliable power from space to anywhere on the globe.

title: Polar Night Energy
linkedin_bio: In partnership with Elisa, implemented an AI-based control system for the world’s largest sand-based thermal energy storage. The AI automatically manages charging of the “sand battery” in real time based on electricity market signals, ensuring stored heat is charged when power is cheapest and discharged when needed​. ​This optimization maximizes efficiency and revenue for the district heating operator while cutting costs.

title: Genesis Ray
linkedin_bio: Uses machine learning combined with geospatial (GIS) analysis to identify optimal locations for large battery energy storage systems. Their AI algorithms evaluate patterns in spatial data – proximity to renewables, grid congestion, land use, etc. – to generate favorability maps pinpointing where storage would yield the most value​. ​This enhances project development by quantitatively scoring sites on technical, economic, and environmental criteria.

title: Moment Energy
linkedin_bio: Developed a proprietary AI-driven battery management system to evaluate and reconfigure retired EV batteries for reuse in stationary storage. The AI algorithms analyze each used battery module’s health and capacity, then optimally group and balance them to form reliable new battery packs​. ​The AI continuously monitors these second-life packs to maximize their lifespan and safety (e.g. predicting cell failures and re-routing power).

title: Oracle
linkedin_bio: Uses behavioral science plus AI analytics to encourage residential customers to save energy. Opower’s cloud platform analyzes smart meter data to create personalized Home Energy Reports (HERs) comparing usage to efficient neighbors and providing tailored tips. These AI-targeted nudges induce consumers to reduce or shift usage (e.g. lowering HVAC during peak). Cumulatively, Opower-driven programs have saved 20+ TWh for utilities​ which equates to a ​roughly 2% consumption reduction per household.

title: Bidgely
linkedin_bio: Pioneered AI-based NILM (Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring) for utilities. Bidgely’s patented algorithms analyze a household’s smart meter power waveform and isolate the “signatures” of major appliances (HVAC, EV, water heater, fridge). ​​Without any plug-level sensors, it can itemize energy usage by appliance and detect inefficient devices. Utilities use these insights to send customers appliance-specific tips (e.g. “your AC is consuming 20% more than peers”) and drive tailored energy savings.

title: Grid4C
linkedin_bio: Provides AI “Predictive Customer Analytics” to help energy retailers design and target optimal rate plans. Grid4C’s machine learning models segment customers by usage patterns and predict their bill outcomes under various tariffs (peak/off-peak, EV plans, etc.). Utilities can then personalize rate recommendations, (for example, identifying which homes would benefit from a time-of-use plan), and forecast the impact of new pricing on load profiles. This data-driven rate matching maximizes customer savings and utility load smoothing.

title: M-KOPA
linkedin_bio: A fintech-energy pioneer providing pay-as-you-go solar systems and microloans via mobile. M-KOPA leverages AI to manage its large prepaid customer base: machine learning models forecast each customer’s loan repayment and energy usage. These AI forecasts let M-KOPA detect early if a user’s payments are slowing (potential default) and intervene with tailored plans. This ensures high repayment rates while extending affordable energy/finance to low-income users who lack formal credit.

title: Brooklyn Microgrid
linkedin_bio: Operated one of the first peer-to-peer energy trading pilots. In the Brooklyn Microgrid project, LO3’s platform let neighbors with rooftop solar sell excess power directly to nearby homes. A combination of blockchain for transaction security and intelligent matching algorithms was used to conduct local energy auctions. The system automatically matched buyers and sellers within the community microgrid and settled trades in 15-minute intervals​. ​This demonstrated how prosumers can use AI + blockchain to create a localized energy marketplace, reducing reliance on the main grid.

For example, in trial communities, solar households automatically sell surplus power (and its associated RECs) to neighbors at dynamic prices​.​The AI forecasts supply/demand and optimizes trades, while blockchain ensures each kWh’s provenance for REC compliance.

It then dispatches these distributed resources in unison to flatten peaks and provide grid services (voltage support, demand response). ​​By optimizing control “at the edges of the grid”​, it maximizes consumer benefits (lower bills via demand charge reduction) and grid reliability.
